rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN S. STORPER VS. STEVEN DILLICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANTS STEVEN DILLICK and STEPHANIE DILLICK's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES DENIED. Defendants are not entitled to recover their attorney's fees. This case has not been resolved on the merits due to Defendant representation that the case was settled; it was not. Therefore, "the claim was not finally denied" and "no action [was] ordered pursuant to section 824(c)" due to the representation made on the record. Defendants are also not the prevailing parties for the purposes of CCP 1717. The case was voluntarily dismissed pursuant to the stipulation entered on the record before the Court. The Court specifically pointed out that Stephanie Dillick was absent at the time of the settlement being put on the record, and Steven Dillick represented that he had authority to bind his wife. Whether or not signed agreement between Mr. and Mrs. Dillick existed (the only other legal way to bind Ms. Dillick) was not within the knowledge of the Court or the opposing counsel; it was only within the knowledge of Mr. Dillick. There was no mutual mistake as urged by the defendants. To the contrary, in the words of the Court of Appeal "We also note the chutzpah of the Dillicks in making [the argument], given Steven Dillick's express assurance on the record to the trial court, unchallenged by any counsel or the court, that he had authority to agree to the settlement for himself and Stephanie, and Stephanie would be bound by its terms." No one can take advantage of his own wrong. CC 3517. = (501/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: MARY GLASPIE FRANK VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to June 18, 2014 per stipulation and order. Moving party and its counsel is ordered again (See CGC-13-529041 June 21, 2013 minute order; CGC-12-523629 July 21, 2013 minute order) to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than May 27, 23014 with cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Any future failure to comply with SFLR 2.6B or C will result in matter being taken off calendar. = (501/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MASTIN et al VS. ANNIE D NGO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Deft Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 21, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO's Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Defendant Leave To Defend DENIED. Defendant fails to sustain burden under CCP 473.5 and CCP 473(b). Proof of Service on file (by the registered process server) is entitled to rebuttable presumption that service was proper. Defendant fails to address in her declaration (1) service attempts from 11/20-12/8 and avoidance thereof; (2) whether a person fitting description in the proof of service lives on the premises; (3) service of summons and complaint by mail; (4) service of request for default. = (501/PHA)
